title: अर्गला स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अर्गला स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्री अर्गलास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य विष्णुः ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री महालक्ष्मीर्देवता।
श्री जगदम्बाप्रीत्यर्थे सप्तशतीपाठांगत्वेन जपे विनियोगः॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

मार्कण्डेय उवाच -

ॐ जयन्ती मंगला काली भद्रकाली कपालिनी।
दुर्गा क्षमा शिवा धात्री स्वाहा स्वधा नमोऽस्तु ते॥१॥

जय त्वं देवि चामुण्डे जय भूतार्तिहारिणि।
जय सर्वगते देवि कालरात्रि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

मधुकैटभविद्राविविधातृवरदे नमः।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥३॥

महिषासुरनिर्नाशविधात्रि वरदे नमः।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥४॥

रक्तबीजवधे देवि चण्डमुण्डविनाशिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥५॥

शुम्भस्यैव निशुम्भस्य धूम्राक्षस्य च मर्दिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥६॥

वन्दिताङ्घ्रियुगे देवि सर्वसौभाग्यदायिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥७॥

अचिन्त्यरूपचरिते सर्वशत्रुविनाशिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥८॥

नतेभ्यः सर्वदा भक्त्या चण्डिके दुरितापहे।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥९॥

स्तुवद्भ्यो भक्तिपूर्वं त्वां चण्डिके व्याधिनाशिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१०॥

चण्डिके सततं ये त्वामर्चयन्तीह भक्तितः।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥११॥

देहि सौभाग्यमारोग्यं देहि मे परमं सुखम्।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१२॥

विधेहि द्विषतां नाशं विधेहि बलमुच्चकैः।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१३॥

विधेहि देवि कल्याणं विधेहि विपुलां श्रियम्।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१४॥

सुरासुरशिरोरत्ननिघृष्टचरणेऽम्बिके।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१५॥

विद्यावन्तं यशस्वन्तं लक्ष्मीवन्तं जनं कुरु।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१६॥

प्रचण्डदैत्यदर्पघ्ने चण्डिके प्रणताय मे।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१७॥

चतुर्भुजे चतुर्वक्त्रसंस्तुते परमेश्वरि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१८॥

कृष्णेन संस्तुते देवि शश्वद्भक्त्या सदाम्बिके।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१९॥

हिमाचलसुतानाथसंस्तुते परमेश्वरि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥२०॥

इन्द्राणीपतिसद्भावपूजिते परमेश्वरि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥२१॥

देवि प्रचण्डदोर्दण्डदैत्यदर्पविनाशिनि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥२२॥

देवि भक्तजनोद्दामदत्तानन्दोदयेऽम्बिके।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥२३॥

पत्नीं मनोरमां देहि मनोवृत्तानुसारिणीम्।
तारिणीं दुर्गसंसारसागरस्य कुलोद्भवाम्॥२४॥

॥ इति श्री अर्गला स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Argala Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Argala-stotra-mantrasya Vishnuh Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Mahalakshmir Devata.
Shri Jagadamba-prityarthe Saptashati-pathangena Jape Viniyogah.

Om Namash-chandikayai.

Markandeya Uvacha -

Om Jayanti Mangala Kali Bhadrakali Kapalini.
Durga Kshama Shiva Dhatri Svaha Svadha Namo'stu Te.||1||

Jaya Tvam Devi Chamunde Jaya Bhutarti-harini.
Jaya Sarvagate Devi Kalaratri Namo'stu Te.||2||

Madhu-kaitabha-vidravi-vidhatri-varade Namah.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||3||

Mahishasura-nirnasha-vidhatri Varade Namah.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||4||

Raktabija-vadhe Devi Chanda-munda-vinashini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||5||

Shumbhasyaiva Nishumbhasya Dhumrakashasya Cha Mardini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||6||

Vanditanghri-yuge Devi Sarva-saubhagya-dayini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||7||

Achintya-rupa-charite Sarva-shatru-vinashini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||8||

Natebhyah Sarvada Bhaktya Chandike Duritapahe.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||9||

Stuvadbhyo Bhakti-purvam Tvam Chandike Vyadhi-nashini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||10||

Chandike Satatam Ye Tvam Archayanteeha Bhaktitah.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||11||

Dehi Saubhagyam Arogyam Dehi Me Paramam Sukham.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||12||

Videhi Dvishatam Nasham Videhi Balam Uchchakaih.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||13||

Videhi Devi Kalyanam Videhi Vipulam Shriyam.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||14||

Surasura-shiro-ratna-nighrishta-charane'mbike.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||15||

Vidyavantam Yashasvantam Lakshmivantam Janam Kuru.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||16||

Prachanda-daitya-darpagne Chandike Pranataya Me.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||17||

Chaturbhuje Chaturvaktra-samstute Parameshwari.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||18||

Krishnena Samstute Devi Shashvad-bhaktya Sadambike.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||19||

Himachala-sutanatha-samstute Parameshwari.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||20||

Indrani-pati-sadbhava-pujite Parameshwari.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||21||

Devi Prachanda-dordanda-daitya-darpa-vinashini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||22||

Devi Bhakta-janod-dama-dattanandodaye'mbike.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||23||

Patnim Manoramam Dehi Manovrittanusarinim.
Tarinim Durga-samsara-sagarasya Kulodbhavam.||24||

|| Iti Shri Argala Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे जयन्ती, मंगला, काली, भद्रकाली, कपालिनी, दुर्गा, क्षमा, शिवा, धात्री, स्वाहा, स्वधा! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे चामुण्डा देवी! आपकी जय हो। हे प्राणियों की पीड़ा हरने वाली! जय हो। हे सर्वव्यापिनी देवी! हे कालरात्रि! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे मधु और कैटभ का विनाश करने वाली! हे वरदायिनी! नमस्कार। मुझे रूप दो, जय दो, यश दो और शत्रुओं का नाश करो।

हे महिषासुर का नाश करने वाली! वरदायिनी! नमस्कार। मुझे रूप दो, जय दो, यश दो और शत्रुओं का नाश करो।

हे सौभाग्य और आरोग्य दो, मुझे परम सुख दो। कल्याण करो, विपुल श्री दो। मुझे विद्यावान, यशस्वी और लक्ष्मीवान बनाओ।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and obstacles
Invokes the blessing of good fortune, health, and supreme happiness
Essential preliminary recitation before Durga Saptashati
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and sufferings of the devotee
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Durga, Argala Stotram (अर्गला स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Drawn from Durga Saptashati (Markandeya Purana), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove all sins and sufferings of the devotee as well as grant beauty (rupam), victory (jayam), and fame (yasho). Additionally, it is known to bestow good fortune, health, and supreme happiness. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. At 24 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Argala Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Argala Stotram from Durga Saptashati in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer for beauty, victory, fame, and destruction of enemies.
relatedSearches: Rupam dehi jayam dehi refrain meaning
Argala Stotram for fame wealth victory

seoFaqs.question: What does the word 'Argala' mean and why is the stotram so named?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Argala' in Sanskrit literally means a 'bolt,' 'latch,' or 'key' — the wooden or iron piece that locks a door from the inside. Hinduism Outlook explains the metaphor: 'just as a lock needs to be opened to gain access to something valuable, the recitation of the Argala Stotram is believed to unlock the blessings and grace of the Divine Mother.' The hymn functions as the spiritual key that unbolts the storehouse of Devi's grace before the main Durga Saptashati recitation begins. Without the Argala Stotram, traditional pandits hold, the Saptashati paath remains incomplete and its phala (fruit) does not fully descend. (Source: Hinduism Outlook Argala Stotram; Greenmesg Argala Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous repeated refrain of the Argala Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Argala Stotram is celebrated for its distinctive refrain: 'Rupam dehi, jayam dehi, yasho dehi, dvisho jahi' — 'Grant me beauty, grant me victory, grant me fame, destroy my enemies.' This four-fold petition, repeated across most of the stotra's verses, makes it one of the most direct boon-asking hymns in the Devi corpus. The economy of the request — covering aesthetics, success, reputation, and protection in a single line — has made it especially popular among householders, students, professionals, and political figures seeking concrete worldly results alongside spiritual progress. (Source: Shlokam.org Argala Stotram; Hindu Mirror Devi Durga Argala Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Argala Stotram fit in the Durga Saptashati recitation sequence?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saptashati paath has a strict ritual order called the Trayanga (three-limb) procedure. Hinduism Outlook describes: 'Trayangam is a method in which we need to chant three prayers — Devi Kavacham, Argala Stotram and Devi Keelakam — followed by Navakshari Mantram.' Specifically: (1) Devi Kavacha first, providing protective armour; (2) Argala Stotram next, unlocking boons; (3) Devi Keelakam, removing the keel/peg that restricts the mantra; (4) Navakshari (nine-syllable Devi mantra) japa; and only then the 700-verse Saptashati. The Argala thus occupies the second slot in this preparatory triad. (Source: Hinduism Outlook Argala Stotram; Sage of Kanchi Devi Mahatmyam reading procedure.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Argala Stotram considered as powerful as the full Saptashati?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — Hinduism Outlook explicitly states: 'Argala Stotra is equally revered like the Saptashati; by reading this with devotion, one obtains difficult-to-obtain boons.' The Devi Mahatmya tradition holds that the seven hundred verses of the Saptashati and the auxiliary stotras (Argala, Kavacha, Keelaka) form a single integrated text where each part contains the potency of the whole. For devotees who lack the time for the full one-to-three-hour Saptashati paath, daily Argala Stotram recitation (about 8–10 minutes) is considered an acceptable equivalent during a 9-day Navratri vrata. (Source: Hinduism Outlook Argala Stotram equal reverence; Drikpanchang Durga Saptashati Argala Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: What victory and protection themes appear throughout the verses?
seoFaqs.answer: Hinduism Outlook summarises that 'both the Argala Stotram lyrics and meaning mainly explain the Supreme Goddess Parameshwari's greatness, her valour, victory over several demons, along with various names of the Devi and her merciful nature toward the devotees.' Specific verses invoke her as Mahishasura-Mardini (slayer of Mahisha), Madhu-Kaitabha-vinashini (destroyer of Madhu and Kaitabha), Shumbha-Nishumbha-mathini (slayer of Shumbha and Nishumbha), and Raktabija-samhantri (annihilator of Raktabija). Each demon represents a specific inner enemy — pride, attachment, lust, or recurring negative thought patterns — that the Goddess uproots in the devotee's life. (Source: Greenmesg Argala Stotram; Litairian Argala Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: When during Navratri is Argala Stotram most powerfully recited?
seoFaqs.answer: While Argala Stotram is recited daily throughout the nine days of Sharadiya Navratri (October) and Vasanta Navratri (March–April), the most powerful occasions are: Maha Ashtami (Day 8, Sandhi Puja at the joint of Ashtami and Navami), Maha Navami (Day 9, when the Devi is in her full victorious form), and Vijayadashami (Day 10, the day of triumph). Hindu Mirror notes that the stotra is the 'perfect Maa Durga Stotram for Navratri.' Tantric practitioners also recite it during the four Gupta Navratris (the secret Navratris in Magha, Ashadha, and other months) for enhanced potency. (Source: Hindu Mirror Devi Durga Argala Stotram for Navratri 2025; Vedadhara Argala Stotram.)
